page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configuration within the E2 instance family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and utilizes an Intel Xeon processor running at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. The hardware allocation consists of 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It features 32 GB of system memory, which establishes a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. This instance does not include any local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations highlight a distinct tradeoff between memory capacity and processing speed. The instance exhibits modest compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks scoring in the bottom 25%. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests place the processor in the bottom 10% tier. Memory performance is more balanced; while sequential read and write bandwidth benchmarks for large blocks fall into the lower 25% tier, the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating in the middle 50% tier. Hardware-assisted cryptography and integer operations are supported via AVX, AES, and BMI2 instruction sets.

The e2-highmem-4 is a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require a larger memory footprint without demanding high-performance CPU throughput. It is well-suited for hosting small-scale databases, running in-memory caching systems like Redis, executing basic text processing pipelines, or serving static web traffic where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational speed.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon CPU running at 2.2 GHz across 2 physical cores, this instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff: while memory capacity is generous, compute performance is modest, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. However, PassMark memory performance remains average. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not demand intense CPU processing. It is best suited for small databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive web applications.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute performance and no local storage.

Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Average memory performance paired with modest CPU benchmark results
Suitable for caching layers, small databases, and memory-heavy web serving
